title: buildah-config: OCI spec v1.0.1 Config:Properties and Annotations
body:
This issue arises in the context of issues #591 and #595.

The current OCI spec (v1.0.1) leaves discretion for which of the [annotations](https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/v1.0.1/annotations.md#pre-defined-annotation-keys) are exposed to the user and where.

The [Config:Properties](https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/v1.0.1/config.md#properties) part of the spec doesn't have an annotations item, nor does it expose all the possible annotation items as config items (some do seem to map but in ways that are inconsistent, e.g. `author` (config:property) and `authors` (annotation)).

@rhatdan : What will buildah do? 

My vote: Expose the annotation items explicitly through `builah config`. Remove `buildah config --labels`

Reasons:
The end user doesn't get any functionality from knowing that `buildah config --version=0.1.2` is an annotation item that maps to **org.opencontainers.image.version**.

It does look like the [v1.0.1 spec suggests deprecating](https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/v1.0.1/annotations.md#back-compatibility-with-label-schema) the `buildah config --labels`.

comments.body: @taqtiqa-mark @nalind recently merged in support for annotations.  Do we need anything additionaly?
